spec_match.rationale: Broken comparison logic: it runs document.execCommand synchronously in the click handler before the setTimeout(0) callback, causing them to check different document states and falsely report that they differ on supported browsers. Additionally, the mouseup-before-click timing race does not apply to external toolbar button clicks.

interactive.rationale: Fully interactive contenteditable rich text editor with functioning bold, italic, underline formatting toggles.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Misaligned: claims that toolbar buttons need v150 timing to read editor selection without setTimeout, which is incorrect because the selection was already committed inside the editor before the toolbar was clicked.

edge_cases.rationale: No feature detection or graceful warnings, and comparison logs report incorrect differences due to timing errors.

references.rationale: Violates repository hard rule: does not contain a link to chromestatus.com/feature/5298387739082752.

design_system.rationale: Nice, clean typography and borders matching the retro design system.

openQuestions.title: Correct the timing and design of toolbar-builder comparison
openQuestions.detail: The toolbar-builder concept is fundamentally flawed: it compares immediate and deferred selection states after calling document.execCommand, causing false mismatches. Also, the mouseup-before-click timing race is irrelevant for toolbar button clicks. Either refactor the toolbar to act as inline formatting handles reacting to editor-level events, or remove/replace this concept with a text selection toolbar (like a medium-style popover toolbar) that spawns immediately on mouseup/click inside the editor.

openQuestions.title: Add ChromeStatus link (hard rule violation)
openQuestions.detail: The page does not contain a link to chromestatus.com/feature/5298387739082752, which violates the repository hard rule that every concept page must include a ChromeStatus link.
